name: AZQ Stage 4 Wave A Checks
purpose: Define task-specific automated checks for Stage 4 Wave A so the Codex runner can evaluate the first bounded Finis review-seam deepening without hardcoding one exact implementation shape into the runner.

baseline_checks.name: compileall
baseline_checks.description: Verify the azq package still compiles after the Stage 4 Wave A change.

baseline_checks.name: stage1_to_stage3_regression_suite
baseline_checks.description: Verify the existing Stage 1 through Stage 3 regression suite still passes while Stage 4 Wave A is being added.

baseline_checks.name: finis_imports
baseline_checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
import azq.finis.cli
import azq.finis.fine
import azq.finis.goals
import azq.finis.goal_manager
import azq.finis.storage
PY
baseline_checks.description: Verify the current public Finis modules still import cleanly.

STAGE4-WAVEA-01.description: Add Finis review path ownership and directory helpers without changing canonical goal storage behavior.

checks.name: review_path_helpers_exist
checks.description: Verify azq.finis.storage owns the new Finis review path seam and deterministic review path helpers.
checks.code:
import azq.finis.storage as storage
for name in ["REVIEWS_DIR", "REVIEW_FILE_SUFFIX", "REVIEW_FILE_GLOB", "ensure_reviews_dir", "list_review_files", "review_file_path"]:
    assert hasattr(storage, name), f"missing review helper: {name}"
assert str(storage.review_file_path("REVIEW_001")).endswith("data/finis/reviews/REVIEW_001.json")

human_checks: Review path ownership should now live in azq/finis/storage.py rather than being rebuilt elsewhere.
The patch should not change existing canonical goal path behavior under data/finis/goals/.
Do not introduce proposals, questions, or intent-package directories in this task.

STAGE4-WAVEA-02.description: Define a minimal Finis review record format and repository helpers.

checks.name: review_record_round_trip
checks.description: Verify the Finis review schema can normalize, serialize, and parse one review record while preserving distinct review-boundary fields.
checks.code:
import azq.finis.storage as storage
record = {
    "review_id": "REVIEW_001",
    "spark_source": "2026-03-20_121334",
    "raw_spark_text": "raw spark text",
    "candidate_goal_text": "Candidate goal text",
    "human_revision_text": "",
    "review_status": "pending",
    "accepted_goal_id": ""
}
normalized = storage.normalize_review_record(record)
text = storage.serialize_review_record(normalized)
parsed = storage.parse_review_record(text)
for field in ["review_id", "spark_source", "raw_spark_text", "candidate_goal_text", "human_revision_text", "review_status", "accepted_goal_id"]:
    assert parsed[field] == normalized[field], field

checks.name: review_repository_helpers_work
checks.description: Verify the Finis review repository helpers can write, load, and list review artifacts in deterministic file order.
checks.code:
import os
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path
import azq.finis.storage as storage
old = Path.cwd()
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
    os.chdir(tmp)
    first = storage.write_review({
        "review_id": "REVIEW_001",
        "spark_source": "spark-1",
        "raw_spark_text": "raw one",
        "candidate_goal_text": "candidate one"
    })
    second = storage.write_review({
        "review_id": "REVIEW_002",
        "spark_source": "spark-2",
        "raw_spark_text": "raw two",
        "candidate_goal_text": "candidate two"
    })
    assert first.is_file()
    assert second.is_file()
    assert storage.load_review("REVIEW_001")["raw_spark_text"] == "raw one"
    all_reviews = storage.load_all_reviews()
    assert [item["review_id"] for item in all_reviews] == ["REVIEW_001", "REVIEW_002"]
    os.chdir(old)

human_checks: The review record format should stay minimal and Wave A scoped, not a disguised proposal or notes schema.
Raw spark text, candidate goal text, optional human revision text, review status, and accepted canonical goal id should remain visibly distinct fields.
The canonical FINIS markdown goal format must remain unchanged in this task.

STAGE4-WAVEA-03.description: Make azq fine materialize durable review artifacts for candidate goals before canonical acceptance.

checks.name: fine_writes_review_artifacts
checks.description: Verify the live azq fine flow can materialize durable Finis review artifacts instead of keeping candidate state only in transient CLI memory.
checks.code:
import contextlib
import io
import json
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path
from unittest import mock
import azq.cli as cli
from azq.finis import storage
class FixedDate:
    @classmethod
    def today(cls):
        class _D:
            def __str__(self):
                return "2026-03-20"
        return _D()
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
    repo_root = Path(tmp)
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    try:
        import os
        os.chdir(repo_root)
        spark_dir = Path("data/scintilla/sparks")
        spark_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        spark_dir.joinpath("2026-03-20_121334.json").write_text(json.dumps([{"spark": "I want to stabilize Finis review boundaries"}]), encoding="utf-8")
        output = io.StringIO()
        with mock.patch("builtins.input", side_effect=["n"]), mock.patch("sys.argv", ["azq", "fine"]), contextlib.redirect_stdout(output):
            cli.main()
        review_files = storage.list_review_files()
        assert review_files, "no review artifacts were written"
        record = storage.load_all_reviews()[0]
        for field in ["spark_source", "raw_spark_text", "candidate_goal_text", "review_status"]:
            assert field in record, field
    finally:
        os.chdir(cwd)

human_checks: azq fine should stay the entrypoint. Do not create a new public Finis command family for this task.
The review artifact should preserve a visible boundary between raw spark text and cleaned candidate goal text.
This task is about durable review state, not yet about promotion or richer question or notes workflows.

STAGE4-WAVEA-04.description: Promote accepted Finis review state into canonical goals through the existing storage seam.

checks.name: accepted_review_promotes_into_canonical_goal
checks.description: Verify accepted review state can promote into canonical goal files while preserving Stage 1 goal fields and recording acceptance back into the review artifact.
checks.code:
import contextlib
import io
import json
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path
from unittest import mock
import azq.cli as cli
from azq.finis import storage
class FixedDate:
    @classmethod
    def today(cls):
        class _D:
            def __str__(self):
                return "2026-03-20"
        return _D()
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
    repo_root = Path(tmp)
    cwd = Path.cwd()
    try:
        import os
        os.chdir(repo_root)
        spark_dir = Path("data/scintilla/sparks")
        spark_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        spark_dir.joinpath("2026-03-20_121334.json").write_text(json.dumps([{"spark": "I want to lock Finis review lineage"}]), encoding="utf-8")
        output = io.StringIO()
        with mock.patch("azq.finis.fine.date", FixedDate), mock.patch("builtins.input", side_effect=["y"]), mock.patch("sys.argv", ["azq", "fine"]), contextlib.redirect_stdout(output):
            cli.main()
        goal = storage.load_goal("FINIS_001")
        assert goal is not None
        assert goal["status"] == "active"
        assert goal["created"] == "2026-03-20"
        assert goal["description"] == ""
        assert goal["derived_from"] == ["2026-03-20_121334"]
        reviews = storage.load_all_reviews()
        assert reviews, "missing review artifact after acceptance"
        assert any(item.get("accepted_goal_id") == "FINIS_001" for item in reviews), reviews
    finally:
        os.chdir(cwd)

human_checks: Promotion should still go through azq.finis.storage goal helpers rather than inventing a second canonical goal write path.
The new review seam should add continuity, not change the existing Stage 1 goal schema or goal markdown format.
Do not broaden this task into manual goal-add refactors or later Stage 4 note surfaces.

STAGE4-WAVEA-05.description: Add a narrow Stage 4 regression slice for the Finis review seam.

checks.name: stage4_wave_a_test_file_exists
checks.description: Verify the Stage 4 Wave A regression test file exists.

checks.name: stage4_wave_a_test_file_covers_review_seam
checks.description: Verify the new Stage 4 regression slice covers the review seam, provenance boundary, and canonical goal promotion anchors.

checks.name: stage4_wave_a_discovery_run
checks.description: Verify the repository unittest discovery run succeeds with the new Stage 4 Wave A regression slice present.

human_checks: The new tests should prove the review seam exists and that Stage 1 canonical goal behavior is preserved, not merely assert that helper names exist.
Keep the regression slice narrow and Wave A scoped. Do not sprawl into later Stage 4 concerns such as questions, usefulness notes, or intent packages.
The Stage 1 baseline test for canonical goal creation through azq fine should remain meaningful rather than being silently invalidated.
